Estrella Mountain Ranch · Goodyear, Arizona

The trail
begins at our
front door.

No trailhead to drive to. Step off the cul-de-sac onto the path: Sonoran desert south, two lakes a flat mile north.

◷ 17737 W Copper Ridge Dr · End of the cul-de-sac
The doorstep

The trail starts at the house.

Walk out the front door, around the end of the cul-de-sac, and you're on dirt. No driving anywhere first.

Red: the home at the cul-de-sac's end. Purple: the trail access, dozens of logged runs fanning into the desert.
Two ways out. North (blue): a flat half-mile wash to the lakes. South/east (clay): past the Bike Park, then east into open desert on the Estrella Foothills Loop toward the Regional Park.

The path goes two ways from the house. North, a flat half-mile gets you to the two lakes, easy enough for anyone, kids and grandparents included. South and east, it heads into open desert and climbs toward the mountains for the people who want a real ride or hike.

That eastern stretch keeps going, all the way out toward the 19,840-acre Estrella Mountain Regional Park. So when you come visit, there's a lazy lake stroll out one direction and a proper desert adventure out the other. Same front door.

0steps on pavement to start
50+miles of community trail
2directions from one door
The lay of the land

Everything, from one pin.

Home, trail access, lakes, bike networks, town errands, and the Regional Park loop, all from one cul-de-sac. The purple line is the wash spine running home to lakes.

Home & trail access Lakes loop Hike / run trails Bike networks Town errands
On foot

Pick your distance. All from the door.

Four outings, from an easy lake lap to a real desert effort. Round-trip from the cul-de-sac.

2miles

The Lakes Lap

Flat · paved49 ft gain★★★★★4.7 / 481 reviews

A few minutes north on the wash path, then a loop around both lakes on smooth paved walkway. Ducks, water, mountain backdrop. Stroller- and dog-friendly, and the single most-used route in the community.

3miles

Lakes + Spine Out-and-Back

Flat · mixed surfaceEasy

The lakes lap plus a push along the community spine before turning back. Flat and forgiving, enough to stretch a 20-minute jog to a half hour. Shade pockets at the lake parks if you start late.

4miles

Desert Connector Loop

Rolling · dirtEasy–moderate

South off the cul-de-sac into the open-desert connectors, looping back through neighborhood paths. Saguaro, washes, quiet. The transition run when the lakes feel too tame.

5+

Blackjack via Estrella Foothills

5.8 mi · 636 ft gainModerate★★★★½4.5 / 515 reviews

Rugged Sonoran terrain, rocky climbs, sweeping valley views. Trails intersect constantly, so download the map. The weekend objective: a desert mountain hike with no car.

The adventures

Roll out the door and keep going.

From the cul-de-sac: warm up on glassy lake pavement, drop into singletrack a reviewer calls "Disneyland for your mountain bike," then point east and ride into open desert until the mountains swallow you. Five rides, each bigger than the last.

The warm-up · paved · easy

The Lake Spin

Glass-smooth pavement around both lakes, flat and fast. Coffee at the marketplace, a lap with the kids, a sunrise easy-day. Straight from the garage.

From the door · any bike, any age
The playground · ~1.5 mi south

FINS Singletrack

Fantasy Island Northern Singletrack: berms, flow, technical features, structures. One rider's verdict, "like Disneyland for your mountain bike." 4.7 stars. A short pedal, then hours of riding.

Beginner flow to expert lines
The skills lab · free

Foothills Bike Park

Public pump track and jump lines, beginner through advanced. Sharpen handling, let the kids session for hours. Sits on the eastern trail's doorstep, so it doubles as the launch pad.

Minutes away · free · all levels
The main event · ~7 mi · the east arm

Estrella Foothills Loop

Past the Bike Park, then east into raw Sonoran desert: "desert riding at its finest." Climbs exposed mountain faces to bench-spots over the whole valley, past the landmark riders call the Circle of Trust, finishing with 3-foot+ jumps if you've got the speed. Forks everywhere, so it's a route you learn and own.

Moderate–technical · ride it tubeless
The expedition · all day · open-ended

Into the Backcountry

Where the Foothills Loop forks deeper, the desert opens into a choose-your-distance day stretching toward the 19,840-acre Estrella Mountain Regional Park and its 33 miles of trail. Unsigned, exposed, real.

From the door · expert · bring everything

The deep desert routes are informal, unmarked, and fork constantly. Load a GPS track (MTB Project and Trailforks both map this network), carry more water than feels reasonable, and ride the big stuff October through April.

The proof

Don't take our word. Read the heat.

Aggregated GPS popularity maps: every logged run and ride from people who train here. Brighter purple means heavier use.

The lakes are the heartbeatThe brightest lines in the whole map wrap both lakes and run the spine south, straight toward Copper Ridge.
The path at our door, lit upThe corridor between Dreamy and Indian Wells, a block from the cul-de-sac, glows purple. A regular route, not a shortcut.
A whole network in motionEstrella Parkway and the village loops trace bright top to bottom. This is how people actually move here.
The big-loop objectiveA ~7-mile saved course out in the Regional Park, the kind of weekend distance waiting just east.

Source: Garmin Connect popularity overlay. Strava's Global Heatmap shows the same pattern from a separate user base. AllTrails traffic ratings agree directionally.

The network on paper

Those thin tan lines? Fifty miles of trail.

The community's own map shows soft-surface paths threading between neighborhoods, along the lakes, and out to the desert edge, separate from the roads.

Full community: tan lines are trail, gray are roads. The wash spine runs north-south down the middle.
Zoomed: paths hug the lakes and parallel the streets, connecting clubs, parks, and schools car-free.
Errands on foot

You can run to coffee.

The lakes spine keeps going to the Estrella Parkway & Elliot marketplace. One-way from home.

~1.8 mi

Safeway

The neighborhood grocery. A backpack turns the chore into a workout.

~1.8 mi

Oasis Bagels

NY-style, 4.6 stars. The post-run reward at the end of the lakes spine.

~1.8 mi

Starbucks

Opens 3:30 AM, exactly when you want to be out in summer.

~1.2 mi

Cafe Bebida

4.7 stars, by the lakes and Welcome Center. Coffee with a view.

The honest part

It's the desert. Plan around the sun.

Summer is hot, and this week proves it: an excessive-heat alert is active, highs near 104°F. Get out at dawn. October through May is the prime season.

Tue
104°
78° lo
Wed
104°
74° lo
Thu
106°
78° lo
Fri
104°
76° lo
Sat
103°
74° lo
Sun
102°
75° lo

Summer: dawn or skip it. Mid-70s before sunrise are runnable; by 9 AM it's done. Carry more water than you think, the desert trails have almost no shade. The lake parks have shade shelters if you need to bail.

October to May is the payoff: 60s and 70s, blue sky, trails busy with the runners and riders who made those heatmaps glow.

"Like Disneyland for your mountain bike."
And it starts at the end of the street.

Estrella Mountain Ranch, Goodyear AZ. Trail data from AllTrails and Garmin Connect; route popularity from aggregated GPS activity; weather current as of early June 2026. Distances and elevation are approximate, GPS over-counts on rolling desert terrain.

Made for sharing with anyone thinking about coming to visit.
